channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now features a variety of third-party integrations, allowing developers to connect their AI agents to platforms like GitHub and Notion. This enhancement aims to facilitate the creation of practical applications using AI.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is set for May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's role in enhancing event experiences. This tradition aims to engage participants ahead of the main event.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem-solving, emphasizing the transition to agentic AI. The event will include technical tracks on various topics, providing developers with essential tools.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, enhancing GPU and NPU acceleration and supporting PyTorch and JAX. This update aims to improve efficiency and security in deploying GenAI applications.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Google Cloud's Wednesday Build Hour is a weekly interactive session designed to help developers enhance their cloud skills through hands-on activities. The program focuses on advanced topics, fostering immediate application of learned skills.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has rolled out updates to improve coding workflows, introducing features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These tools aim to make AI a more effective collaborator for developers.

item.title: Introducing Finish Changes and Outlines, now available in Gemini Code Assist extensions on IntelliJ and VS Code
item.description: Google's Gemini Code Assist now includes features like Finish Changes and Outlines in IntelliJ and VS Code, designed to reduce manual coding efforts. These tools enhance code comprehension and streamline development processes.

item.title: Manage AI costs with Amazon Bedrock Projects
item.description: Amazon Bedrock Projects allows users to manage AI inference costs by attributing them to specific workloads. This feature helps in analyzing and optimizing costs through AWS tools.

item.title: Iran cyber actors disrupting US water, energy facilities, FBI warns
item.description: The FBI warns of Iranian cyber actors targeting US water and energy facilities, leading to operational disruptions. This escalation highlights ongoing cybersecurity threats to critical infrastructure.

item.title: Nutanix thinks some Azure cloud desktops belong on-prem to make them usable
item.description: Nutanix is collaborating with Microsoft to bring Azure cloud desktops on-premises, leveraging its virtualization expertise to enhance usability. This move aims to improve the performance of cloud-based applications.

item.title: I can’t help rooting for tiny open source AI model maker Arcee
item.description: Arcee, a small U.S. startup, has developed a high-performing open-source language model that is gaining traction among OpenClaw users. This showcases the potential of smaller players in the AI space.

item.title: Cloudflare, GoDaddy team up to curb AI bot brigades
item.description: Cloudflare and GoDaddy are collaborating to create standards that will help differentiate between trustworthy AI agents and harmful bots. This partnership aims to protect web content from unwanted AI interactions.

item.title: AWS CEO: It's funny when people ask me if AI is overhyped
item.description: AWS CEO Matt Garman downplays concerns about AI being overhyped, suggesting that the industry should focus on realistic expectations. His comments come amid discussions at the Human[X] conference in San Francisco.

item.title: Hundreds of orgs compromised daily in Microsoft device code phishing attacks
item.description: A Microsoft device-code phishing attack is compromising hundreds of organizations daily, utilizing AI and automation to bypass traditional security measures. This highlights the evolving threat landscape in cybersecurity.

item.title: Your AirPods are gross. This $6 tool will keep them sparkling clean
item.description: A simple $6 tool is recommended for cleaning AirPods, highlighting the importance of maintaining personal tech hygiene. Regular cleaning can enhance the longevity and performance of earbuds.

item.title: Intel gets trapped in Elon’s reality distortion field as it joins in megafab delusions
item.description: Intel has joined Elon Musk's ambitious Terafab initiative, which aims to significantly increase semiconductor production for future AI applications. This reflects the ongoing hype around AI and its associated technologies.

item.title: LG C6 vs. LG C5: I compared two generations of OLED TVs, and it was pretty darn close
item.description: A comparison between LG's C6 and C5 OLED TVs reveals that while the C6 is a refreshed model, the differences may not justify an upgrade for all users. This analysis helps consumers make informed purchasing decisions.

item.title: Anthropic Teams Up With Its Rivals to Keep AI From Hacking Everything
item.description: Anthropic is collaborating with over 45 organizations, including major tech companies, on Project Glasswing to enhance AI cybersecurity using its Claude Mythos Preview model. This initiative aims to prevent AI from exploiting vulnerabilities.

item.title: Firmus, the ‘Southgate’ AI data center builder backed by Nvidia, hits $5.5B valuation
item.description: Firmus, an AI data center builder backed by Nvidia, has achieved a valuation of $5.5 billion after raising $1.35 billion in six months. This reflects the growing demand for AI infrastructure.

item.title: I swapped my MacBook Air with a Snapdragon X Elite Extreme laptop - here's how they compared
item.description: A comparison between a MacBook Air and an Asus Snapdragon X Elite Extreme laptop reveals varying opinions on ultraportability and performance, showcasing the diverse options available in the laptop market.

item.title: Nutanix brings its K8s to bare metal because hardware matters again
item.description: Nutanix announced at its .NEXT conference that it is expanding its Kubernetes compatibility to bare metal servers, emphasizing the importance of hardware in today's market. This move aims to address current supply chain challenges in acquiring hardware.

item.title: Google Chrome's vertical tabs are here: How to opt in and use the new Reading Mode
item.description: Google Chrome has introduced vertical tabs, allowing users to manage their tabs more efficiently. This feature aims to enhance user experience by providing a new way to organize and access tabs.

item.title: Apple, Google, and Microsoft join Anthropic's Project Glasswing to defend world's most critical software
item.description: Tech giants Apple, Google, and Microsoft have joined Anthropic's Project Glasswing, a collaborative effort to identify vulnerabilities in critical software using AI. This initiative is likened to a modern-day Manhattan Project for AI security.

item.title: Love window snapping on Linux? You should try a tiling window manager - here's why
item.description: This article discusses the benefits of using tiling window managers on Linux, which can enhance productivity by allowing users to manage windows more effectively. It provides recommendations for six popular tiling managers.

item.title: Building real-time conversational podcasts with Amazon Nova 2 Sonic
item.description: The article explores how to create real-time conversational podcasts using Amazon Nova 2 Sonic, showcasing its capabilities in generating engaging dialogues between AI hosts. This demonstrates advancements in audio generation technology.

item.title: Text-to-SQL solution powered by Amazon Bedrock
item.description: A guide on building a text-to-SQL solution using Amazon Bedrock, which translates natural language business queries into database commands. This tool aims to streamline data access and improve decision-making.

item.title: Why I returned to Enlightenment Linux after 30 years: Testing the new Elive beta
item.description: The article reviews the new Elive beta, a Debian-based Linux distribution that revives the old-school Enlightenment desktop environment while offering a modern user interface. The author expresses a desire for enhanced features like voice control.

item.title: Verifying and optimizing post-quantum cryptography at Amazon
item.description: Amazon discusses its approach to verifying and optimizing post-quantum cryptography, focusing on automated reasoning to balance security, performance, and maintainability. This is crucial as quantum computing advances.

item.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
item.description: In celebration of National Robotics Week, NVIDIA highlights advancements in physical AI, showcasing how robots are transforming various industries through improved learning and simulation technologies.

item.title: A Skeptic’s Thoughts on BioCell Liquid Collagen
item.description: The author shares a personal journey from skepticism to enthusiasm regarding BioCell Liquid Collagen, reflecting on its benefits and effectiveness. This highlights the growing interest in health and wellness products.

item.title: Anthropic taps Google and Broadcom for yet more AI chips as revenue run rate tops $30B
item.description: Anthropic's annual revenue run rate has surged to over $30 billion, up from $9 billion, following a partnership expansion with Google and Broadcom for AI chips. This growth signifies a strong demand for AI technologies and positions Anthropic as a major player in the industry.

item.title: claude-status 0.3.0
item.description: Claude Code has released version 0.3.0, featuring a new status line that is visually appealing and informative, designed to be cross-platform with zero dependencies. This update enhances user experience for developers using Claude Code.

item.title: Web Design Trends 2026: The Definitive Guide
item.description: A guide on web design trends for 2026 outlines 14 key trends including bento grids, scroll-driven animation, and AI imagery. This resource serves as a valuable reference for designers looking to stay ahead in the evolving digital landscape.

item.title: altergo 0.4.0
item.description: The latest release of Claude Code, version 0.4.0, allows users to switch identities without losing their thoughts, enhancing the flexibility of the tool. This feature aims to improve user experience by providing seamless transitions.

item.title: Medvi, the AI-powered telehealth company, is fueled by ads from doctors who don't appear to exist
item.description: Medvi, an AI-powered telehealth company, faces lawsuits over its marketing tactics that included seemingly fictitious doctors. This controversy raises ethical concerns about transparency in AI-driven healthcare marketing.

item.title: stats-compass-mcp 0.3.9
item.description: The MCP server has been updated to expose stats-compass-core tools to various large language models, including ChatGPT and Claude. This development aims to enhance the capabilities of these AI models.

item.title: Web Development Trends 2026: What Every Designer-Developer Needs to Know
item.description: A comprehensive overview of web development trends for 2026 highlights 14 key advancements such as AI-assisted coding and edge deployment. This guide is essential for developers to adapt to the future of web technology.

item.title: AI-backed super PAC expands into multiple GOP primaries after claiming early wins
item.description: The pro-AI super PAC, Leading the Future, has announced a $1.5 million campaign to support GOP candidates in critical midterm primaries, following claimed early successes. This move illustrates the growing intersection of AI technology and politics.

item.title: Anonymous Sources Detail Sam Altman’s Alleged Untrustworthiness in New Report
item.description: A report reveals anonymous sources questioning Sam Altman's trustworthiness, comparing him to notorious figures like Bernie Madoff. This raises concerns about leadership credibility in the tech industry.

item.title: OpenAI Calls For Robot Taxes, Public Wealth Fund, and 4-Day Workweek To Tackle AI Disruption
item.description: OpenAI has proposed significant policy changes to address societal disruptions caused by AI, including robot taxes and a public wealth fund. These recommendations aim to create a framework for managing the impact of advanced AI technologies.

item.title: Show HN: Luigis-meter – Claude Code Max quota in your statusline
item.description: Luigis-meter is a new tool that helps users track their Claude Code usage in real-time, providing a statusline-native solution for monitoring session limits. This tool is significant for developers who rely on Claude Code for their projects.

item.title: rectified-flow-pytorch 0.9.9
item.description: The rectified-flow-pytorch 0.9.9 update introduces enhancements to the Rectified Flow model implementation in PyTorch. This update is important for developers working on generative models.

item.title: npc-gym added to PyPI
item.description: npc-gym has been added to PyPI, offering a replacement for gymnasium to train hybrid LLM and ML agents through games and neuroevolution. This is significant for researchers in AI training methodologies.

item.title: Microsoft Brings Microsoft 365 Copilot Co-Creation to Word for iPhone
item.description: Microsoft has rolled out co-creation features for Microsoft 365 Copilot in Word for iPhone, allowing users to collaborate more effectively on documents. This update enhances productivity for mobile users.

item.title: piighost 0.5.1
item.description: The piighost 0.5.1 update provides middleware for anonymizing personally identifiable information in AI agent conversations, integrating with LangChain. This is crucial for maintaining privacy in AI interactions.

item.title: nishant-ragkit added to PyPI
item.description: The nishant-ragkit library has been added to PyPI, designed for document-based retrieval-augmented generation (RAG). This tool is significant for developers focusing on document processing in AI.

item.title: euler-train 2.4.1
item.description: Euler-train 2.4.1 is a lightweight file-based experiment logger for PyTorch, aimed at simplifying experiment tracking for developers. This tool is beneficial for improving workflow efficiency.

item.title: jztree 1.0.1
item.description: Jztree 1.0.1 is a framework designed for Morton z-order trees with a CUDA backend, enhancing performance for spatial data processing. This is important for developers working with large datasets.

item.title: jztree-cu13 1.0.1
item.description: The jztree-cu13 1.0.1 update introduces a CUDA 13 backend for the jztree framework, improving performance and compatibility. This update is relevant for developers using CUDA for AI tasks.

item.title: OmniOutliner Essentials and Pro 6.1
item.description: OmniOutliner Essentials and Pro 6.1 adds over 20 new Shortcuts to its Pro edition, enhancing usability for macOS users. This update is significant for those who rely on OmniOutliner for organization.
